segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Did the doctors find out how the woman died?

translation.text: 医生们查明这名女子的死因了吗？

segments.text: The mummy of an Egyptian woman who died in 800 B.C.

translation.text: 一具公元前800年去世的埃及女子木乃伊。

segments.text: The mummy is that of Shepenmut, who was once a singer in the temple of Thebes.

translation.text: 这具木乃伊是舍彭穆特，她曾是底比斯神庙的一名歌女。

segments.text: As there were strange marks on the x-ray plates taken of the mummy, doctors have been trying to find out whether the woman died of a rare disease.

translation.text: 由于木乃伊的X光片上有一些奇怪的斑点，医生们一直试图查明这名女子是否死于一种罕见疾病。

segments.text: The only way to do this was to operate.

translation.text: 要弄清这一点，唯一的办法就是动手术。

segments.text: The operation, which lasted for over four hours, proved to be very difficult because of the hard resin which covered the skin.

translation.text: 手术持续了四个多小时；由于皮肤覆盖着坚硬的树脂，手术非常困难。

segments.text: The doctors removed a section of the mummy and sent it to a laboratory.

translation.text: 医生们从木乃伊上取下一部分，送往实验室。

segments.text: They also found something which the x-ray plates did not show, a small wax figure of the god Duamutef.

translation.text: 他们还发现了X光片上没有显示的东西：一尊杜阿穆特夫神的小蜡像。

segments.text: This god, which has the head of a cow, was normally placed inside a mummy.

translation.text: 这位牛头神通常被放在木乃伊体内。

segments.text: The doctors have not yet decided how the woman died.

translation.text: 医生们仍未确定这名女子的死因。

segments.text: They feared that the mummy would fall to pieces when they cut it open, but fortunately, this has not happened.

translation.text: 他们原本担心切开木乃伊时它会散成碎片，但幸运的是，这并没有发生。

segments.text: The mummy successfully survived the operation.

translation.text: 这具木乃伊成功挺过了手术。
